title: datiscaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank Datiscaceae refers to a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of water-loving plants that are native to the Old World and are characterized by their distinctive flowers and fruits. This family is part of the larger group of angiosperms, which includes most types of flowering plants. Members of the Datiscaceae family are often aquatic or semi-aquatic, and they are known for their adaptations to life in or near water. The plants within this family are typically herbaceous or shrub-like in growth habit and are found in a variety of aquatic and semi-aquatic habitats.
